free_text: - Measurement of the activation cross section of
(N,Np+d) reactions for lantanide isotopes in the
Fusion neutron source of JAERI, deutron
ion beam is 150 Milli-A, energy 350 KeV
.Pneumatic sample transport system was used to return
samples from irradiation to measurement position
Stack target irradiation. Each sample was
sandwitched between two aluminium or niobium
.Samples were irradiated at the angles from zero to
150 degrees relative to deutron beam. Effective
neutron energy was determined by Nb/Zr method.
The typical neutron fluence rate at 10 cm distance
-counting loss owing to true and random coincidence
-deviation in detection efficiency owing to the sample
thickness - about 2% for sample thickness 1.35 mm
-self-absorption of gamma-rays in the irradiated sample
.Total erros (err-t) consists of quadratic sum contri-
Max statistical error of gamma-ray peak count
Max low energy neutron contribution error
Max neutron fluence rate fluctuation error
Max error from contribution of interfering
Reference cross section error of Al-27(N,a
Max error for absolute gamma-ray intensity
